subject: Product Management
title: The PM Triad
description: Learn The PM Triad in one minute.

hook.text: Every product team has a sacred triangle: a product manager, a designer, and an engineering lead.

buildup.text: The PM owns the 'what' and 'why.' The designer owns the 'experience.' The engineer owns the 'how.'

discovery.text: When the triad works, decisions are fast and products are cohesive. Each person covers a blind spot of the others.

twist.text: Most dysfunction in product teams traces back to a broken triad — unclear ownership, ego, or missing trust.

climax.text: Marty Cagan calls the triad 'the atomic unit of product.' Break it, and everything downstream breaks too.

punchline.text: Three roles, one product. Alignment is everything.

quiz.question: What is the PM triad?
options: A product manager, designer, and engineering lead working as one unit
Three product managers sharing the role
